verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted trypsin-like serine protease abundantly produced and secreted by prostate luminal epithelium, detectable at high levels in seminal plasma and blood. It lacks a canonical transmembrane domain. However, a 2025 Clinical Cancer Research study systematically confirmed KLK2 cell-surface expression in prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP) by FACS and confocal microscopy co-localizing with EpCAM, and in patient-derived mCRPC tumor cells — establishing an oncogenic-state ecto-pool. Multiple KLK2-directed therapeutics (bispecific T-cell engager pasritamig/JNJ-78278343, CAR-T, actinium-225 radioligand) have entered clinical trials engaging KLK2 on the cell surface of prostate cancer cells. This qualifies as cell_state_induced (cancer-restricted surface display of a nominally secreted protein), not a stable baseline surface localization.
